title: skopeo 404 against nexus oss docker registry
body:
I am using [nexus oss](https://www.sonatype.com/nexus-repository-oss) (3.25.0-03) to host a private docker registry and have configured it as per the [documentation](https://help.sonatype.com/repomanager3/formats/docker-registry/hosted-repository-for-docker-%28private-registry-for-docker%29).

When I try and use skopeo inspect I am getting a 404 against the image I am trying to query:
```
skopeo inspect docker://mynexus.com/docker-releases/hello-world:linux
FATA[0000] Error parsing image name "docker://mynexus.com/docker-releases/hello-world:linux": error pinging docker registry mynexus.com: invalid status code from registry 404 (Not Found)
```

From the debug I can see its trying to look for v2 at the root level:
```
DEBU[0000] GET https://mynexus.com/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://mynexus.com/v2/ status 404
```

Whereas in the nexus setup, v2 exists after the repository name, e.g:
`mynexus.com/docker-releases/v2`

Is there some config I can use to amend where it is trying to ping?

comments.body:
Thanks for opening the issue!

Can you ping the registry, or does it work with Docker? My suspicion is that something is off in the setup.

Thanks for getting back to me. I can do a docker pull from my nexus successfully:
`docker pull mynexus.com/docker-releases/hello-world:linux`

Checking my nexus request.log I can see two entries from when I have ran skopeo inspect:
```
[06/Aug/2020:14:06:44 +0000] "GET /v2/ HTTP/1.1" 404 - 1991 1 "Go-http-client/1.1"
[06/Aug/2020:14:06:45 +0000] "GET /v1/_ping HTTP/1.1" 404 - 1991 1 "Go-http-client/1.1"
```

> From the debug I can see its trying to look for v2 at the root level:
> 
> ```
> DEBU[0000] GET https://mynexus.com/v2/
> DEBU[0000] Ping https://mynexus.com/v2/ status 404
> ```
> 
> Whereas in the nexus setup, v2 exists after the repository name, e.g:
> `mynexus.com/docker-releases/v2`

That’s not how the API (https://github.com/docker/distribution/blob/master/docs/spec/api.md#put-manifest ) works. It is not possible to operate a registry with that protocol that does not use the root namespace on the host name, if only because the image syntax (`docker://mynexus.com/docker-releases/hello-world:linux`) has no way to specify whether the URL to access should be e.g. `mynexus.com/v2/docker-releases/hello-world/manifests/linux` or `mynexus.com/docker-releases/v2/hello-world/manifests/linux`.

Compare the Docker request log; I’m fairly sure you will see a `/v2/` access as well.
